```json
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "//! &#96;RunResult&#96;, &#96;ExecContext&#96;, and &#96;BuiltinError&#96; for command execution."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "use super::super::vfs::Vfs;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "/// Result of running a pipeline: continue the REPL loop or exit."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "pub enum RunResult {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "    Continue,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "    Exit,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "/// Execution context: VFS and standard streams for one command."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "pub struct ExecContext\u003c'a\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "    pub vfs: \u0026'a mut Vfs,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "    pub stdin: \u0026'a mut dyn std::io::Read,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "    pub stdout: \u0026'a mut dyn std::io::Write,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "    pub stderr: \u0026'a mut dyn std::io::Write,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "/// Error from builtin execution (redirect or VFS failure, unknown command)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "#[derive(Debug)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "pub enum BuiltinError {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "    UnknownCommand(String),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "    RedirectRead,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "    RedirectWrite,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "    CdFailed,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "    MkdirFailed,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "    CatFailed,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "    TouchFailed,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "    LsFailed,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "    ExportFailed,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "    SaveFailed,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "    TodoLoadFailed,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "    TodoSaveFailed,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "    TodoArgError,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "    TodoDataError,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "    /// rustup not found in PATH"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "    RustupNotFound,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "    /// cargo not found in PATH"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "    CargoNotFound,"
          },

          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "    SandboxExportFailed,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "    /// Sandbox sync (temp dir back to VFS) failed"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "    SandboxSyncFailed,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "}"

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 29179,
            "text": "The 'CargoNotFound' error likely stems from ExportFailed with NotFound, not from find_in_path returning None. The flow is: find_in_path locates cargo, returns its path. Then run_in_export_dir is called with that path. The spawn failure likely occurs because the cargo binary at the path returned by find_in_path is either missing or has a broken shebang pointing to a non-existent interpreter. The spawn failure likely stems from a missing cargo binary, broken symlink, or non-existent working directory. I confirmed the export directory structure is correct for the project path. I traced copy_tree_to_host and confirmed it resolves paths correctly but may be creating export directories with incorrect names. "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29180
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29181,
            "text": "The export directory structure is flat, creating export_dir/hello/Cargo.toml instead of the expected nested structure. The VFS path resolution is incorrect - it should resolve to the hello directory node, but the export structure doesn't match the expected nested layout. "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29182
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29183,
            "text": "**Correcting vfs path**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29184
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29185,
            "text": "I'm fixing the VFS path resolution to correctly map host and export directories. I'm fixing the VFS path resolution to correctly match t_export_root with copy_tree_to_host's actual export directory structure. I'm clarifying the host export root calculation for VFS paths with multiple segments. I'm correcting the host export root calculation to use the last segment of the VFS path. "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29186
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29187,
            "text": "I'm implementing the host export root calculation using the last segment of VFS path. \u003c/details\u003e\u003c/think\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29188
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29189,
            "text": "_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29190
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29191
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29192,
            "text": "确认为 &#96;host_export_root&#96; 的计算与 &#96;copy_tree_to_host&#96; 的实际布局不一致。"

      "codeBlock": {

          {
            "lineNumber": 26834,
            "text": "Let me re-read the impl plan and the current codebase to implement Task 1 correctly."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26835
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26836,
            "text": "Task 1: VFS 导出到临时目录"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26837,
            "text": "- Create a new module (e.g. sandbox.rs or isolate.rs) in devshell"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26838,
            "text": "- Create temp dir under std::env::temp_dir() with name devshell_\u003cuuid\u003e, permission 0o700"
          },

            "lineNumber": 26839,
            "text": "- Use VFS copy_tree_to_host(vfs_path, host_dir) to export the subtree at vfs.cwd()"
          },

            "text": "I need to check:"

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26845,

          },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "**步骤**："
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "1. **Linux**：导出完成后，对临时目录调用 &#96;std::fs::remove_dir&#96; 的**父级下的该目录名**（即 unlink 目录条目）；父进程通过 &#96;std::fs::File::open&#96; 在 unlink 前打开目录（或使用 &#96;openat&#96; 等，依 std 能力），保留 &#96;File&#96;（即 fd）。子进程 cwd 通过 &#96;/proc/self/fd/\u003cn\u003e&#96;（n 为 fd 编号）传入，或使用 &#96;rustix&#96;/&#96;nix&#96; 等库的 &#96;fexecve&#96;/&#96;openat&#96; 类 API 将 fd 作为 cwd；若 std 的 &#96;Command::current_dir&#96; 只接受路径，则 Linux 首期可回退为「不 unlink、仅随机路径 + 0700」，在文档中注明「严格 fd-only 需后续依赖 os 扩展」。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "2. **非 Linux**：不 unlink；保留 &#96;PathBuf&#96;，子进程使用 &#96;Command::current_dir(\u0026path)&#96;。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "3. 抽象：提供 &#96;run_in_export_dir(path: Option\u003c\u0026Path\u003e, fd: Option\u003c\u0026File\u003e, program: \u0026str, args: \u0026[String], stdin/stdout/stderr)&#96;：若 fd 可用且平台支持则以 fd 为 cwd，否则用 path；执行 &#96;program&#96;、&#96;args&#96;，透传 stdio。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "**验证**：Linux：创建目录后 unlink，断言路径不再可访问、fd 仍可读写目录内容。非 Linux：断言子进程 cwd 为导出目录。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "**文件**：&#96;sandbox.rs&#96;（或 &#96;isolate.rs&#96;）；可选依赖 &#96;rustix&#96;/&#96;nix&#96; 用于 fd-as-cwd（若 std 无法满足）。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "**说明**：若首期在 Linux 上实现「fd 作为 cwd」成本高，可统一采用「随机路径 + 0700」，在实现计划与文档中注明「fd-only 为后续增强」。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": "## Task 3：从临时目录同步回 VFS"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "**目标**：将导出目录中的变更（相对导出根的新增、修改、删除）写回 VFS 对应子树。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "**步骤**："
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "1. 遍历临时目录（通过保留的 path 或 fd）；对每个文件：计算相对导出根的路径，在 VFS 中对应路径写入内容（若为新增则创建父目录与文件，若为修改则覆盖）。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "2. 删除：遍历 VFS 当前子树，若宿主机导出目录中已不存在该相对路径的节点，则从 VFS 中删除（或实现「差分」：先列出导出目录全部相对路径，再与 VFS 子树对比，删除 VFS 中有而导出目录没有的）。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "3. 实现时注意：导出目录可能含 &#96;target/&#96;、&#96;.git&#96; 等大或无关目录；设计约定是否全部同步回 VFS 或排除部分目录（如 &#96;target/&#96;）；首期可全部同步，后续可加排除列表。"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 57
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 33
          }
        },

        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3590371608734131
    }
  ]
}
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Adding host file utilities**

I'm implementing BOM stripping and UTF-8/UTF-16 decoding utilities for host file reading. I'm implementing UTF-16 LE/BE BOM detection and decoding using `String::from_utf16` for Windows Notepad compatibility. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


确认：仓库里没有针对 Windows 乱码的专门更新；导出/同步路径已用二进制 `read`/`write`，理论上字节一致。Windows 上常见问题是宿主脚本/编辑器使用 UTF-8 BOM 或 UTF-16，而 `read_to_string`/`from_utf8` 未处理 BOM 或 UTF-16。正在添加 `host_text` 模块并在脚本加载处使用它。

## 结论（之前）

**没有**单独为 Windows 做过「防乱码」更新。和 Linux 一样，沙箱里 **VFS ↔ 临时目录** 一直是 **`std::fs::read` / `write` 按字节**，一般不会把 `.rs` 内容解坏。

Windows 上更容易出问题的是：**把脚本 / JSON 当「文本」读进来**时仍用 **`read_to_string`（只认 UTF-8）**，而记事本等会存成 **UTF-8 BOM** 或 **UTF-16（带 BOM）**，轻则首字节怪字符，重则整段乱码或读失败。

## 本次已做的更新

新增 **`crates/todo/src/devshell/host_text.rs`**，并在这些路径统一用 **`read_host_text` / `decode_host_text_bytes`**：

| 场景 | 行为 |
|------|------|
| `dev_shell -f script.dsh` | 支持 UTF-8（含 BOM）、UTF-16 LE/BE（含 BOM） |
| REPL `source` / `.`（读**宿主**文件） | 同上 |
| 脚本里 `source`（读宿主文件） | 同上 |
| 从 VFS 读脚本内容 | 用 **`script_text_from_vfs_bytes`**，同样可处理 BOM / UTF-16（若你曾把带 BOM 的内容写进 VFS） |
| **`load_todos` → `.todo.json`** | 改为经 `host_text`，避免带 BOM 的 JSON 读坏 |

**未改动的部分（刻意保持）**：`cargo`/`rustup` 的导出、同步、VFS `cat` 等仍是**二进制管道**，不经过这套文本解码。

**文档**：在 **`docs/design.md`** 的持久化小节加了一段说明（宿主文本 vs 沙箱字节路径）。

本地已跑过 **`cargo test -p xtask-todo-lib`** 与 **`clippy -D warnings`**。

若乱码仍出现在 **终端显示**（代码页不是 UTF-8），那是控制台编码问题，可在 Windows 终端里用 **UTF-8 代码页**（例如 `chcp 65001`）或把终端设为 UTF-8；与本次文件读入修复是两层问题。

---

_**User**_

@\home\ars\.cursor\projects\home-ars-richard-2026-pvm-2-xtask-todo\terminals\9.txt:1026-1038 还是有问题。请分析

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Investigating execution error**

I'm investigating why the binary fails to execute with 'Permission denied' error. 

**Checking file permissions**

I found that the binary likely lacks execute permissions due to how Cargo handles file creation in the sandboxed environment. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


分析中：`Permission denied (os error 13)` 在 `cargo run` 尝试执行 `target/debug/hello` 时出现。常见原因是 `/tmp` 被挂载为 `noexec`，导致无法在临时导出目录中执行刚构建的二进制文件。

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Checking exec permissions**

I'm investigating why the binary fails with 'Permission denied' after building with cargo, likely due to missing execute permissions or noexec on /tmp. I found that the binary is being built with 755 permissions, but the issue likely stems from AppArmor/SELinux blocking execution in /tmp. </details></think>

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="ripgrep_raw_search"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **ripgrep_raw_search**</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


定位到两个问题：
1. 根本原因：许多 Linux 将 `/tmp` 挂载为 `noexec`，在 `std::env::temp_dir()` 下 `cargo build` 成功但无法 `exec` `target/debug/hello` → EACCES (13)。
2. 误导性错误：`cargo` 非零退出被映射为 `SandboxExportFailed`，REPL 打印 `error: SandboxExportFailed`，与真实原因不符。

正在修复：将导出根目录改到用户缓存（并支持 `DEVSHELL_EXPORT_BASE`），并引入 `RustToolNonZeroExit` + `Display`。



_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Investigating test failures**

The initial test failure was due to an assertion error in `run_with_todo_list_json_in_temp_dir`, not poison from prior failures. The test failure is likely due to parallel tests racing on cwd with conflicting locks, not due to serde_json formatting differences. 

The test failure occurs because one test panics while holding a cwd lock, poisoning the mutex and causing subsequent tests to fail with PoisonError. The test panic while holding cwd lock may be caused by `run_with` not restoring cwd on panic, leading to subsequent PoisonError failures. 

I found that `run_with_todo_list_json_in_temp_dir` may not restore cwd if assertion fails, but `run_with` itself shouldn't panic. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


修复 `cwd_test_lock`：在 `assert!` 失败时会在仍持有 `Mutex` 时 panic，导致 mutex 被毒化。缩小锁的作用域，在释放锁后再断言。



_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


## 原因分析

1. **`Permission denied (os error 13)`**  
   沙箱把工程导出到 **`std::env::temp_dir()`**（在 Linux 上经常是 **`/tmp`**）。不少发行版/加固策略会给 **`/tmp` 挂 `noexec`**：  
   - `cargo build` 可以写 `target/debug/hello`  
   - 但 **`exec` 该二进制会被内核拒绝** → 正是你看到的错误  

2. **`error: SandboxExportFailed`**（误导）  
   `cargo` 以 **101** 退出时，原来被统一映射成 `SandboxExportFailed`，和「导出失败」无关，只是 **`cargo run` 子进程失败**。

## 已做修改

| 项 | 说明 |
|----|------|
| **导出根目录** | 新增 `devshell_export_parent_dir()`：优先 **`XDG_CACHE_HOME/cargo-devshell-exports`**，否则 **`~/.cache/cargo-devshell-exports`**，Windows 用 **`%LOCALAPPDATA%/cargo-devshell-exports`**，最后才退回 `temp_dir()`。可用 **`DEVSHELL_EXPORT_BASE`** 覆盖。 |
| **`export_vfs_to_temp_dir` / `export-readonly`** | 都改为在上述父目录下建唯一子目录，并 `create_dir_all`。 |
| **错误类型** | `cargo`/`rustup` 非零退出 → **`RustToolNonZeroExit { program, code }`**；REPL/脚本里用 **`Display`** 输出，例如：`error: cargo exited with status 101`。 |
| **文档** | 已更新 `README.md`、`docs/design.md`、`docs/dev-container.md`。 |
| **测试** | 全 crate 共用一个 **`test_support::cwd_mutex()`**，避免多处 `chdir` 并行抢 cwd；`PATH`/`DEVSHELL_EXPORT_BASE` 相关锁在 poison 时用 **`into_inner()`** 恢复。 |

请再试：`cargo run --bin cargo-devshell`，在 `/projects/hello` 里执行 **`cargo run`**。若仍异常，可显式设：

```bash
export DEVSHELL_EXPORT_BASE="$HOME/tmp-devshell-exec"
```

并确认该目录所在文件系统**未**挂 `noexec`。

---

@\home\ars\.cursor\projects\home-ars-richard-2026-pvm-2-xtask-todo\terminals\9.txt:1031-1039 还有问题。请继续分析。或者切换到完整的虚拟机的方案。

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Checking execute permissions**

I'm investigating why the binary lacks execute permissions despite switching export directories. The binary likely needs explicit chmod +x after build, or the filesystem is blocking execution. The export directory might be correct but sync from VFS overwrote files without execute permission. I'm investigating whether `copy_tree_to_host` uses `std::fs::write` and if previous builds in VFS are overwritten during sync. I found that files created by rustc should be user-owned with +x permissions to avoid rebuilds during sync. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


分析问题：`~/.cache` 已避免 `/tmp` 的 `noexec`，但 EACCES 仍出现。很可能原因：从 VFS 同步回宿主时，`target/debug/hello` 经 `std::fs::write` 写入会丢失 Unix 执行位（0644）；下次导出再 `cargo run` 时 cargo 认为无需重建，会尝试执行无 `+x` 的二进制 → Permission denied。正在验证并实现导出后在 `target/` 下为 ELF 等可执行文件恢复 `chmod +x`。

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Adding unix execute bits**

I'm implementing `restore_execute_bits_for_build_artifacts` to fix permissions on build artifacts. 

**Restoring execute bits**

I'm restoring execute bits on build artifacts using chmod 755 for directories and files. I'm skipping shebang scripts since they're rare in target and the chmod fix addresses the common case. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


确认：`std::fs::write` 会创建 `0644` 文件；VFS 同步把 `target/debug/hello` 存成字节后再导出会丢失执行位，`cargo` 若跳过重建会尝试执行无 `+x` 的文件 → EACCES。正在在运行 `cargo`/`rustup` 之前为 `target/` 下的 ELF 文件恢复 `0755`。

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


## 进一步分析结论

在已经改用 **`~/.cache/cargo-devshell-exports`** 之后仍然出现 **`Permission denied (os error 13)`**，更常见的原因不是 `noexec`，而是：

**VFS ↔ 宿主同步只用字节 + `std::fs::write`，Unix 上新建文件默认是 `0644`，没有执行位。**

流程是：

1. 第一次在导出目录里 `cargo build`，`rustc` 会生成带 **`+x`** 的 `target/debug/hello`。  
2. `sync` 把该二进制**按内容**写回 VFS（不保存 mode）。  
3. 下一次再 `export` 时，`copy_tree_to_host` 用 **`write`** 把 `hello` 写回磁盘 → 变成 **`0644` 的 ELF**。  
4. `cargo run` 发现产物仍“最新”，**不再链接**，直接去 **`execve`** → 文件不可执行 → **EACCES (13)**。

这与 “Finished … 0.01s” 很快、像是**没重新链接**的现象一致。

## 已做修改

在 **`run_rust_tool`** 里，导出完成后、启动 **`cargo`/`rustup` 之前**，对工程根下的 **`target/`** 做递归扫描：凡是文件头为 **ELF 魔数** `0x7F ELF` 的常规文件，一律 **`chmod 0755`**（仅 Unix；Windows 仍为空操作）。

- 实现：`sandbox.rs` 中 `restore_execute_bits_for_build_artifacts` + `host_file_starts_with_elf_magic` / `restore_elf_execute_bits_under_target`  
- 单测：`restore_execute_bits_makes_elf_under_target_executable`  
- 说明：`docs/dev-container.md` 已补充；`docs/design.md` 里 §2.5 已有对应描述  

请 **`cargo build --bin cargo-devshell` 后再跑 `cargo-devshell`**，在 `/projects/hello` 里再试 **`cargo run`**。

---

## 关于「完整虚拟机」方案

那是另一条产品线量级：要管镜像、网络、卷挂载、资源、生命周期等，不会放在当前这个 **进程内 VFS + 宿主 cargo** 的 devshell 里做默认实现。若你将来要 **强制** 与宿主隔离的构建环境，更适合单独做 **「在 VM/容器里跑整套 cargo」** 的集成，而不是把 QEMU/KVM 塞进本仓库的默认路径。

若 chmod 之后仍 EACCES，再排查：**AppArmor/SELinux**、**`mount | grep noexec`**（对 `~/.cache` 所在分区）、以及用 **`DEVSHELL_EXPORT_BASE`** 指到确认可执行的目录（例如工程所在的真实磁盘路径）。

---

_**User**_

@superpowers 使用头脑风暴技能，帮我将当前的隔离方案，修改为使用完整的虚拟机的方案。

---

```json
          {
            "lineNumber": 69,
            "text": "|---------|---------|"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70,
            "text": "| \"This is just a simple question\" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71,
            "text": "| \"I need more context first\" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 72,
            "text": "| \"Let me explore the codebase first\" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 73,
            "text": "| \"I can check git/files quickly\" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 74,
            "text": "| \"Let me gather information first\" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 75,
            "text": "| \"This doesn't need a formal skill\" | If a skill exists, use it. |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 76,
            "text": "| \"I remember this skill\" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 77,
            "text": "| \"This doesn't count as a task\" | Action = task. Check for skills. |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78,
            "text": "| \"The skill is overkill\" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79,
            "text": "| \"I'll just do this one thing first\" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80,
            "text": "| \"This feels productive\" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81,
            "text": "| \"I know what that means\" | Knowing the concept ≠ using the skill. Read it. |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 82
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83,
            "text": "## Skill Priority"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 85,
            "text": "When multiple skills could apply, use this order:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 87,
            "text": "1. **Process skills first** (brainstorming, debugging) - these determine HOW to approach the task"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88,
            "text": "2. **Implementation skills second** - these guide execution"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90,
            "text": "\"Let's build X\" → brainstorming first, then implementation skills."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91,
            "text": "\"Fix this bug\" → systematic-debugging first, then domain-specific skills."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93,
            "text": "## Skill Types"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95,
            "text": "**Rigid** (TDD, debugging): Follow exactly. Don't adapt away discipline."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 95
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 46
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.cursor/rules/superpowers-bootstrap.mdc",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3935491144657135
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7006,
            "text": "            \"text\": \"# Superpowers Bootstrap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7007,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7008,
            "text": "          {"
          },

          },

            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 3,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7013,
            "text": "            \"text\": \"You have superpowers.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7014,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7015,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7020,
            "text": "            \"text\": \"**IMPORTANT: The using-superpowers skill content is included below. It is ALREADY LOADED - you are currently following it. Do NOT read \\\"using-superpowers\\\" again - that would be redundant.**\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7021,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7026,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 7,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7027,
            "text": "            \"text\": \"## How to Access Skills in Cursor\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7028,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7029,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7030,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 8"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7031,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7032,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7033,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 9,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7034,
            "text": "            \"text\": \"Use the \u0026#96;Read\u0026#96; tool on skill files located at \u0026#96;.cursor/skills/superpowers/skills/{skill-name}/SKILL.md\u0026#96; (relative to this workspace).\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7035,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7036,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7038,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7039,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7040,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 11,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7041,
            "text": "            \"text\": \"**Available skills:**\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7042,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7043,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7044,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 12,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7045,
            "text": "            \"text\": \"- \u0026#96;brainstorming\u0026#96; - Interactive design refinement before implementation\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7046,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7047,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7048,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 13,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7049,
            "text": "            \"text\": \"- \u0026#96;test-driven-development\u0026#96; - RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7050,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7051,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7052,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 14,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7053,
            "text": "            \"text\": \"- \u0026#96;systematic-debugging\u0026#96; - 4-phase root cause process\""
          },
          {

            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7055,
            "text": "          {"

          },

          {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "\u003c!-- OPENSPEC:START --\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "# OpenSpec Instructions"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "These instructions are for AI assistants working in this project."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "Always open &#96;@/openspec/AGENTS.md&#96; when the request:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "- Mentions planning or proposals (words like proposal, spec, change, plan)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "- Introduces new capabilities, breaking changes, architecture shifts, or big performance/security work"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "- Sounds ambiguous and you need the authoritative spec before coding"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "Use &#96;@/openspec/AGENTS.md&#96; to learn:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "- How to create and apply change proposals"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "- Spec format and conventions"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "- Project structure and guidelines"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "Keep this managed block so 'openspec update' can refresh the instructions."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "\u003c!-- OPENSPEC:END --\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "\u003cskills_system priority=\"1\"\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "## Available Skills"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "\u003c!-- SKILLS_TABLE_START --\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "\u003cusage\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "When users ask you to perform tasks, check if any of the available skills below can help complete the task more effectively. Skills provide specialized capabilities and domain knowledge."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "How to use skills:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "- Invoke: &#96;npx openskills read \u003cskill-name\u003e&#96; (run in your shell)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "  - For multiple: &#96;npx openskills read skill-one,skill-two&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "- The skill content will load with detailed instructions on how to complete the task"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "- Base directory provided in output for resolving bundled resources (references/, scripts/, assets/)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "Usage notes:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "- Only use skills listed in \u003cavailable_skills\u003e below"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "- Do not invoke a skill that is already loaded in your context"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "- Each skill invocation is stateless"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "\u003c/usage\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "\u003cavailable_skills\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "\u003cskill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "\u003cname\u003ealgorithmic-art\u003c/name\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "\u003cdescription\u003eCreating algorithmic art using p5.js with seeded randomness and interactive parameter exploration. Use this when users request creating art using code, generative art, algorithmic art, flow fields, or particle systems. Create original algorithmic art rather than copying existing artists' work to avoid copyright violations.\u003c/description\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "\u003clocation\u003eproject\u003c/location\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "\u003c/skill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "\u003cskill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": "\u003cname\u003ebrand-guidelines\u003c/name\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "\u003cdescription\u003eApplies Anthropic's official brand colors and typography to any sort of artifact that may benefit from having Anthropic's look-and-feel. Use it when brand colors or style guidelines, visual formatting, or company design standards apply.\u003c/description\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "\u003clocation\u003eproject\u003c/location\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "\u003c/skill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "\u003cskill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "\u003cname\u003ecanvas-design\u003c/name\u003e"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 55
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./AGENTS.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3249143958091736
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 93,
            "text": "## Skill Types"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95,
            "text": "**Rigid** (TDD, debugging): Follow exactly. Don't adapt away discipline."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97,
            "text": "**Flexible** (patterns): Adapt principles to context."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99,
            "text": "The skill itself tells you which."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 100
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "text": "## User Instructions"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103,
            "text": "Instructions say WHAT, not HOW. \"Add X\" or \"Fix Y\" doesn't mean skip workflows."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 103
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 92
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.cursor/rules/superpowers-bootstrap.mdc",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.2954047918319702
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 110,
            "text": "\u003cdescription\u003eCreate new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.\u003c/description\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111,
            "text": "\u003clocation\u003eproject\u003c/location\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112,
            "text": "\u003c/skill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 113
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114,
            "text": "\u003cskill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115,
            "text": "\u003cname\u003eslack-gif-creator\u003c/name\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116,
            "text": "\u003cdescription\u003eKnowledge and utilities for creating animated GIFs optimized for Slack. Provides constraints, validation tools, and animation concepts. Use when users request animated GIFs for Slack like \"make me a GIF of X doing Y for Slack.\"\u003c/description\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,
            "text": "\u003clocation\u003eproject\u003c/location\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118,
            "text": "\u003c/skill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120,
            "text": "\u003cskill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 121,
            "text": "\u003cname\u003etemplate\u003c/name\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122,
            "text": "\u003cdescription\u003eReplace with description of the skill and when Claude should use it.\u003c/description\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 123,
            "text": "\u003clocation\u003eproject\u003c/location\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 124,
            "text": "\u003c/skill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 125
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 126,
            "text": "\u003cskill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 127,
            "text": "\u003cname\u003etheme-factory\u003c/name\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 128,
            "text": "\u003cdescription\u003eToolkit for styling artifacts with a theme. These artifacts can be slides, docs, reportings, HTML landing pages, etc. There are 10 pre-set themes with colors/fonts that you can apply to any artifact that has been creating, or can generate a new theme on-the-fly.\u003c/description\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 129,
            "text": "\u003clocation\u003eproject\u003c/location\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 130,
            "text": "\u003c/skill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 131
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 132,
            "text": "\u003cskill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 133,
            "text": "\u003cname\u003eweb-artifacts-builder\u003c/name\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 134,
            "text": "\u003cdescription\u003eSuite of tools for creating elaborate, multi-component claude.ai HTML artifacts using modern frontend web technologies (React, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui). Use for complex artifacts requiring state management, routing, or shadcn/ui components - not for simple single-file HTML/JSX artifacts.\u003c/description\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 135,
            "text": "\u003clocation\u003eproject\u003c/location\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 136,
            "text": "\u003c/skill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 137
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 138,
            "text": "\u003cskill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 139,
            "text": "\u003cname\u003ewebapp-testing\u003c/name\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 140,
            "text": "\u003cdescription\u003eToolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing browser screenshots, and viewing browser logs.\u003c/description\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 141,
            "text": "\u003clocation\u003eproject\u003c/location\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 142,
            "text": "\u003c/skill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 143
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 144,
            "text": "\u003cskill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 145,
            "text": "\u003cname\u003exlsx\u003c/name\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 146,
            "text": "\u003cdescription\u003e\"Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like \\\"the xlsx in my downloads\\\") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.\"\u003c/description\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 147,
            "text": "\u003clocation\u003eproject\u003c/location\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 148,
            "text": "\u003c/skill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 149
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 150,
            "text": "\u003c/available_skills\u003e"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 150
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 109
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./AGENTS.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.28180235624313354
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 6809,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6810,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6811,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6812,
            "text": "            \"text\": \"| \\\"This feels productive\\\" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6813,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6814,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6815,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 81,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6816,
            "text": "            \"text\": \"| \\\"I know what that means\\\" | Knowing the concept ≠ using the skill. Read it. |\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6817,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6818,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6819,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 82"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6820,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6821,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6822,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 83,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6823,
            "text": "            \"text\": \"## Skill Priority\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6824,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6825,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6826,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 84"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6827,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6828,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6829,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 85,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6830,
            "text": "            \"text\": \"When multiple skills could apply, use this order:\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6831,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6832,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6833,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 86"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6834,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6835,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6836,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 87,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6837,
            "text": "            \"text\": \"1. **Process skills first** (brainstorming, debugging) - these determine HOW to approach the task\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6838,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6839,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6840,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 88,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6841,
            "text": "            \"text\": \"2. **Implementation skills second** - these guide execution\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6842,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6843,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6844,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 89"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6845,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6846,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6847,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 90,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6848,
            "text": "            \"text\": \"\\\"Let's build X\\\" → brainstorming first, then implementation skills.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6849,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6850,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6851,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 91,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6852,
            "text": "            \"text\": \"\\\"Fix this bug\\\" → systematic-debugging first, then domain-specific skills.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6853,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6854,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6855,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 92"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6856,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6857,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6858,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 93,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6859,
            "text": "            \"text\": \"## Skill Types\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6860,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6861,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6862,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 94"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6863,
            "text": "          },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6864,
            "text": "          {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6865,
            "text": "            \"lineNumber\": 95,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6866,
            "text": "            \"text\": \"**Rigid** (TDD, debugging): Follow exactly. Don't adapt away discipline.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6867,
            "text": "          }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6868,
            "text": "        ],"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6869,
            "text": "        \"range\": {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6870,

          },

          },

          },
          {

            "text": "        },"
          },
          {

          },
          {

            "text": "\u003csummary\u003eTool use: **ripgrep_raw_search**\u003c/summary\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6477
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6478,
            "text": "\u003c/details\u003e\u003c/tool-use\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6479
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6480,
            "text": "_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6481
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6482,
            "text": "\u003cthink\u003e\u003cdetails\u003e\u003csummary\u003eThought Process\u003c/summary\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6483
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6484,
            "text": "Let me read the superpowers-bootstrap rule to find where the brainstorming skill is, and also search for any skill file that describes the brainstorming process."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6485,
            "text": "\u003c/details\u003e\u003c/think\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6486
          },
          {

            "text": "_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_"

    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 79,
            "text": "│   └── Markdown instructions"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80,
            "text": "└── Bundled Resources (optional)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81,
            "text": "    ├── scripts/    - Executable code for deterministic/repetitive tasks"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 82,
            "text": "    ├── references/ - Docs loaded into context as needed"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83,
            "text": "    └── assets/     - Files used in output (templates, icons, fonts)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 85
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86,
            "text": "#### Progressive Disclosure"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 87
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88,
            "text": "Skills use a three-level loading system:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89,
            "text": "1. **Metadata** (name + description) - Always in context (~100 words)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90,
            "text": "2. **SKILL.md body** - In context whenever skill triggers (\u003c500 lines ideal)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91,
            "text": "3. **Bundled resources** - As needed (unlimited, scripts can execute without loading)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93,
            "text": "These word counts are approximate and you can feel free to go longer if needed."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95,
            "text": "**Key patterns:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96,
            "text": "- Keep SKILL.md under 500 lines; if you're approaching this limit, add an additional layer of hierarchy along with clear pointers about where the model using the skill should go next to follow up."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97,
            "text": "- Reference files clearly from SKILL.md with guidance on when to read them"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98,
            "text": "- For large reference files (\u003e300 lines), include a table of contents"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 100,
            "text": "**Domain organization**: When a skill supports multiple domains/frameworks, organize by variant:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102,
            "text": "cloud-deploy/"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103,
            "text": "├── SKILL.md (workflow + selection)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 104,
            "text": "└── references/"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 105,
            "text": "    ├── aws.md"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106,
            "text": "    ├── gcp.md"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107,
            "text": "    └── azure.md"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 108,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 109,
            "text": "Claude reads only the relevant reference file."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 110
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111,
            "text": "#### Principle of Lack of Surprise"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 113,
            "text": "This goes without saying, but skills must not contain malware, exploit code, or any content that could compromise system security. A skill's contents should not surprise the user in their intent if described. Don't go along with requests to create misleading skills or skills designed to facilitate unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or other malicious activities. Things like a \"roleplay as an XYZ\" are OK though."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115,
            "text": "#### Writing Patterns"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,
            "text": "Prefer using the imperative form in instructions."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119,
            "text": "**Defining output formats** - You can do it like this:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;markdown"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 121,
            "text": "## Report structure"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122,
            "text": "ALWAYS use this exact template:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 123,
            "text": "# [Title]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 124,
            "text": "## Executive summary"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 125,
            "text": "## Key findings"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 126,
            "text": "## Recommendations"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 127,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 128
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 78
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.claude/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.26733461022377014
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "name: skill-creator"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "description: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "# Skill Creator"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "A skill for creating new skills and iteratively improving them."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "At a high level, the process of creating a skill goes like this:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "- Decide what you want the skill to do and roughly how it should do it"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "- Write a draft of the skill"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "- Create a few test prompts and run claude-with-access-to-the-skill on them"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "- Help the user evaluate the results both qualitatively and quantitatively"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "  - While the runs happen in the background, draft some quantitative evals if there aren't any (if there are some, you can either use as is or modify if you feel something needs to change about them). Then explain them to the user (or if they already existed, explain the ones that already exist)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "  - Use the &#96;eval-viewer/generate_review.py&#96; script to show the user the results for them to look at, and also let them look at the quantitative metrics"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "- Rewrite the skill based on feedback from the user's evaluation of the results (and also if there are any glaring flaws that become apparent from the quantitative benchmarks)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "- Repeat until you're satisfied"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "- Expand the test set and try again at larger scale"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "Your job when using this skill is to figure out where the user is in this process and then jump in and help them progress through these stages. So for instance, maybe they're like \"I want to make a skill for X\". You can help narrow down what they mean, write a draft, write the test cases, figure out how they want to evaluate, run all the prompts, and repeat."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "On the other hand, maybe they already have a draft of the skill. In this case you can go straight to the eval/iterate part of the loop."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "Of course, you should always be flexible and if the user is like \"I don't need to run a bunch of evaluations, just vibe with me\", you can do that instead."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "Then after the skill is done (but again, the order is flexible), you can also run the skill description improver, which we have a whole separate script for, to optimize the triggering of the skill."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 28
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.claude/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.2650837004184723
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "name: frontend-design"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "license: Complete terms in LICENSE.txt"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "This skill guides creation of distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces that avoid generic \"AI slop\" aesthetics. Implement real working code with exceptional attention to aesthetic details and creative choices."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "The user provides frontend requirements: a component, page, application, or interface to build. They may include context about the purpose, audience, or technical constraints."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "## Design Thinking"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "Before coding, understand the context and commit to a BOLD aesthetic direction:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "- **Purpose**: What problem does this interface solve? Who uses it?"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "- **Tone**: Pick an extreme: brutally minimal, maximalist chaos, retro-futuristic, organic/natural, luxury/refined, playful/toy-like, editorial/magazine, brutalist/raw, art deco/geometric, soft/pastel, industrial/utilitarian, etc. There are so many flavors to choose from. Use these for inspiration but design one that is true to the aesthetic direction."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "- **Constraints**: Technical requirements (framework, performance, accessibility)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "- **Differentiation**: What makes this UNFORGETTABLE? What's the one thing someone will remember?"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "**CRITICAL**: Choose a clear conceptual direction and execute it with precision. Bold maximalism and refined minimalism both work - the key is intentionality, not intensity."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "Then implement working code (HTML/CSS/JS, React, Vue, etc.) that is:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "- Production-grade and functional"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "- Visually striking and memorable"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "- Cohesive with a clear aesthetic point-of-view"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "- Meticulously refined in every detail"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "## Frontend Aesthetics Guidelines"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 28
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.claude/skills/frontend-design/SKILL.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.26000338792800903
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "3. What's the expected output format?"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "4. Should we set up test cases to verify the skill works? Skills with objectively verifiable outputs (file transforms, data extraction, code generation, fixed workflow steps) benefit from test cases. Skills with subjective outputs (writing style, art) often don't need them. Suggest the appropriate default based on the skill type, but let the user decide."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "### Interview and Research"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "Proactively ask questions about edge cases, input/output formats, example files, success criteria, and dependencies. Wait to write test prompts until you've got this part ironed out."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "Check available MCPs - if useful for research (searching docs, finding similar skills, looking up best practices), research in parallel via subagents if available, otherwise inline. Come prepared with context to reduce burden on the user."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "### Write the SKILL.md"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "Based on the user interview, fill in these components:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66,
            "text": "- **name**: Skill identifier"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67,
            "text": "- **description**: When to trigger, what it does. This is the primary triggering mechanism - include both what the skill does AND specific contexts for when to use it. All \"when to use\" info goes here, not in the body. Note: currently Claude has a tendency to \"undertrigger\" skills -- to not use them when they'd be useful. To combat this, please make the skill descriptions a little bit \"pushy\". So for instance, instead of \"How to build a simple fast dashboard to display internal Anthropic data.\", you might write \"How to build a simple fast dashboard to display internal Anthropic data. Make sure to use this skill whenever the user mentions dashboards, data visualization, internal metrics, or wants to display any kind of company data, even if they don't explicitly ask for a 'dashboard.'\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68,
            "text": "- **compatibility**: Required tools, dependencies (optional, rarely needed)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69,
            "text": "- **the rest of the skill :)**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71,
            "text": "### Skill Writing Guide"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 72
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 73,
            "text": "#### Anatomy of a Skill"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 75,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 76,
            "text": "skill-name/"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 77,
            "text": "├── SKILL.md (required)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78,
            "text": "│   ├── YAML frontmatter (name, description required)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79,
            "text": "│   └── Markdown instructions"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80,
            "text": "└── Bundled Resources (optional)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81,
            "text": "    "
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 4,
            "line": 80
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 52
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.claude/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.25197553634643555
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "name: canvas-design"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "description: Create beautiful visual art in .png and .pdf documents using design philosophy. You should use this skill when the user asks to create a poster, piece of art, design, or other static piece. Create original visual designs, never copying existing artists' work to avoid copyright violations."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "license: Complete terms in LICENSE.txt"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "These are instructions for creating design philosophies - aesthetic movements that are then EXPRESSED VISUALLY. Output only .md files, .pdf files, and .png files."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "Complete this in two steps:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "1. Design Philosophy Creation (.md file)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "2. Express by creating it on a canvas (.pdf file or .png file)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "First, undertake this task:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "## DESIGN PHILOSOPHY CREATION"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "To begin, create a VISUAL PHILOSOPHY (not layouts or templates) that will be interpreted through:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "- Form, space, color, composition"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "- Images, graphics, shapes, patterns"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "- Minimal text as visual accent"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "### THE CRITICAL UNDERSTANDING"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "- What is received: Some subtle input or instructions by the user that should be taken into account, but used as a foundation; it should not constrain creative freedom."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "- What is created: A design philosophy/aesthetic movement."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "- What happens next: Then, the same version receives the philosophy and EXPRESSES IT VISUALLY - creating artifacts that are 90% visual design, 10% essential text."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "Consider this approach:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "- Write a manifesto for an art movement"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "- The next phase involves making the artwork"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "The philosophy must emphasize: Visual expression. Spatial communication. Artistic interpretation. Minimal words."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "### HOW TO GENERATE A VISUAL PHILOSOPHY"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "**Name the movement** (1-2 words): \"Brutalist Joy\" / \"Chromatic Silence\" / \"Metabolist Dreams\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "**Articulate the philosophy** (4-6 paragraphs - concise but complete):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "To capture the VISUAL essence, express how the philosophy manifests through:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "- Space and form"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "- Color and material"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "- Scale and rhythm"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "- Composition and balance"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "- Visual hierarchy"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "**CRITICAL GUIDELINES:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "- **Avoid redundancy**: Each design aspect should be mentioned once. Avoid repeating points about color theory, spatial relationships, or typographic principles unless adding new depth."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "- **Emphasize craftsmanship REPEATEDLY**: The philosophy MUST stress multiple times that the final work should appear as though it took countless hours to create, was labored over with care, and comes from someone at the absolute top of their field. This framing is essential - repeat phrases like \"meticulously crafted,\" \"the product of deep expertise,\" \"painstaking attention,\" \"master-level execution.\""
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 48
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.claude/skills/canvas-design/SKILL.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.24276429414749146
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 74,
            "text": "\u003cdescription\u003e\"Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.\"\u003c/description\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 75,
            "text": "\u003clocation\u003eproject\u003c/location\u003e"
          },
          {

Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.\u003c/description\u003e"
          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86,
            "text": "\u003cdescription\u003eA set of resources to help me write all kinds of internal communications, using the formats that my company likes to use. Claude should use this skill whenever asked to write some sort of internal communications (status reports, leadership updates, 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQs, incident reports, project updates, etc.).\u003c/description\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 87,
            "text": "\u003clocation\u003eproject\u003c/location\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88,
            "text": "\u003c/skill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90,
            "text": "\u003cskill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91,
            "text": "\u003cname\u003emcp-builder\u003c/name\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92,
            "text": "\u003cdescription\u003eGuide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).\u003c/description\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93,
            "text": "\u003clocation\u003eproject\u003c/location\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94,
            "text": "\u003c/skill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95
          },

          },
          {
```
